mnemonics: CHECKED: Can't Handle Errors? Keep Executing, Dear! (Checked exceptions must be handled or declared, Unchecked/RuntimeExceptions don't)
keyPoints: Java has two main types of exceptions: Checked (compile-time) and Unchecked (RuntimeExceptions)
Common built-in exceptions include NullPointerException, ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException, IOException, and ClassCastException
All exceptions inherit from the Throwable class, with Error and Exception as the two main subclasses
commonMistakes: Catching Exception instead of a more specific exception type, which can mask unexpected errors
Using exceptions for normal program flow control instead of simple conditional checks
Empty catch blocks that swallow exceptions without any handling or logging
